song.title: O Christ He Is The Fountain
song.author: Anne Ross Cousin
song.lyrics:
[V1] 1) O Christ, He is the fountain, The deep sweet well of life: Its living streams I’ve tasted Which save from grief and strife.|| And to an ocean fulness, His mercy doth expand; His grace is all-sufficient As by His wisdom planned. [V2] 2) O I am my Beloved’s, And my Beloved’s mine! He brings a poor vile sinner Into His "house of wine."|| I stand upon His merit; I know no other stand. I’m hidden in His presence And held by His own hand. [V3] 3) The Bride eyes not her garment, But her dear Bridegroom’s face; I will not gaze at glory, But on my King of grace:|| Not at the crown He giveth, But on His pierced hand; The Lamb is all the glory, And my eternal stand!
[E] 1) O Christ, He is the fountain, The deep sweet well of life: Its living streams I’ve tasted Which save from grief and strife. And to an ocean fulness, His mercy doth expand; His grace is all-sufficient As by His wisdom planned. 2) O I am my Beloved’s, And my Beloved’s mine! He brings a poor vile sinner Into His "house of wine." I stand upon His merit; I know no other stand. I’m hidden in His presence And held by His own hand. 3) The Bride eyes not her garment, But her dear Bridegroom’s face; I will not gaze at glory, But on my King of grace: Not at the crown He giveth, But on His pierced hand; The Lamb is all the glory, And my eternal stand!
